rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical chlorosulfuration or chlorosulfoxidation of unactivated olefins with thiophenols and HCl, with selectivity switched by operating conditions
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of thiophenol to thiyl radical and subsequent anodic oxidation of carbon radical to carbocation (and sulfide to sulfonium/oxonium for sulfoxide formation); cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: thiyl radical (ArS•) from anodic oxidation of p-toluenethiol
carbon-centered radical from thiyl addition to cyclohexene
carbocation from anodic oxidation of carbon radical
sulfonium intermediate (IV, resonance form of III)
cationic sulfur radical V from anodic oxidation of sulfide
sulfonium VII after further anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes ArSH to thiyl radical I (−H+), which adds to cyclohexene to give C-radical II; II is further oxidized at the anode to carbocation III (≡ sulfonium IV), which is trapped by Cl− to give sulfide 3a. Under prolonged electrolysis, 3a is anodically oxidized to a sulfur-centered cation radical V, trapped by H2O to oxonium VI, then oxidized to VII and deprotonated to sulfoxide 4a; cathode reduces 2H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down; TEMPO–sulfide adduct 5 detected by HRMS, indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Bis(4-methylphenyl) disulfide (6)
probe_reagents.observation: Isolated in 60% after 3 h; when used in place of ArSH gave 4a in 82%, supporting thiyl radical formation via disulfide

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O
probe_reagents.observation: 18O-labeled sulfoxide 7 obtained (7:4a = 69:31, 76%), showing the sulfoxide oxygen comes from H2O (in concentrated HCl), not O2

notes: Selectivity between chlorosulfuration (3) and chlorosulfoxidation (4) is controlled by electrolysis time/conditions: shorter time gives sulfide 3a, longer time leads to further anodic oxidation to sulfoxide 4a with H2O as the oxygen source. Carbocation III and sulfonium IV are presented as resonance forms of the same intermediate.
